title: genista lobelii subsp. longipes
intro: Genista lobelii subsp. longipes, also known as the Long-stalked Spanish broom or Genista, is a plant species within the Fabaceae family and is native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds, which have been found to have potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing. Genista lobelii subsp. longipes is traditionally used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to offer various health benefits, including antioxidant and immune-boosting properties. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of its medicinal uses, and its preparations should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
